title: R.A.G.E.
subtitle: Windows Automation Agent (AI Desktop Assistant)
problem: Executing complex, multi-step desktop operations from open-ended natural language inputs requires secure execution, real-time feedback loops, and error recovery without manual human intervention.
approach: Developed a fully autonomous Windows agent using a ReAct (Reason+Act) feedback loop. It translates natural language goals into system actions, intercepts active window handles, reads errors, and corrects them. Built background daemons for file sorting, clipboard tracking, and system events with an SQLite safety ledger.
outcomes: Autonomous execution of multi-step desktop goals (compiling daily briefs, layout tiling, etc.)
Adaptive ReAct feedback loop for real-time error recovery and window tracking
Proactive background daemons for downloads, clipboard monitoring, and notification interception
SQLite transaction ledger for instant action rollbacks and safety filters

title: Advanzia AutoLend
subtitle: Autonomous Credit Limit Optimization
problem: Traditional credit limit adjustments rely on static rules that fail to adapt to real-time risk signals, leaving portfolio profit on the table while exposing lenders to uncontrolled default rates.
approach: Implemented a Dueling Double DQN-based risk optimization engine inspired by existing research on dynamic credit limit optimization, combined with a Cox Proportional Hazard Model to estimate default probabilities. Architected a high-throughput microservices backend (Spring Boot, FastAPI) leveraging Kafka event streams and Redis caching.
outcomes: Dynamic credit limit adjustment optimized using PyTorch reinforcement learning
Microservices backend benchmarking throughput at 5,000+ requests/min under 300ms latency
Immutable double-entry ledger architecture with complete audit trails
Simulation pipeline modeling 10,000+ synthetic users for policy pre-training

title: DOC-OC
subtitle: Automated Marksheet Extraction System
problem: Manual transcription of academic marksheets across different education boards is slow, error-prone, and doesn't scale — taking ~10 minutes per document.
approach: Engineered a multi-board extraction pipeline utilizing OpenCV, custom YOLOv8 object detection, and Hugging Face Table Transformers to automate structured data parsing for administrative workflows. Built a custom structure-retaining OCR engine (via LLM Whisperer) integrated with strict fraud-mitigation checks to ensure data integrity across consecutive semesters. Developed a React/TypeScript and async FastAPI portal backed by a secure MySQL database.
outcomes: Multi-board extraction pipeline parsing structured tabular and field data
Custom structure-retaining OCR engine integrated with strict fraud-mitigation checks
30x reduction in document processing time (from 10 minutes to ~30 seconds)
FastAPI and React/TypeScript web portal backed by a secure MySQL database

title: Pustak Tracker
subtitle: Library Management System
problem: Small and institutional libraries need affordable, mobile-friendly management systems that don't require expensive barcode hardware.
approach: Architected a full-stack library solution using Flutter for mobile and Flask for backend. Integrated mobile-vision barcode scanning to replace hardware scanners, with secure JWT authentication and automated fine calculation.
outcomes: Replaced hardware barcode scanners, saving ₹4,000 in infrastructure
Secure JWT-based authentication
Real-time book tracking and automated fine calculation
Mobile-first design with Flutter

title: CampusIntel Phase 2
subtitle: Unified Campus Intelligence System
problem: Educational institutions lack unified platforms that combine campus automation, AI-powered student assistance, and document processing into a single, accessible system.
approach: Built a comprehensive multi-service platform with AI chat powered by FAISS-based RAG, smart library management, automated OCR-based document processing, and a real-time communication layer — all deployed as containerized microservices.
outcomes: Live production deployment on Render
Multi-service architecture (4 backend services)
AI-powered campus assistant with RAG retrieval
Top 3 in HackTheWinter offline final round
